search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk/guidance/ng12 dysphagia weight loss upper GI
candidate_sources_rejected: PPI or H. pylori treatment dose tables
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Medicine selection and doses were outside scope.
Referral and investigation decisions must remain clinician-led and recommendation-qualified.
Non-official diet advice was not accepted.
population_applicability: Partial: CG184 applies to adults aged 18 and over; NG12 warning recommendations retain age and symptom-combination qualifiers. The workflow has no age filter.
setting_applicability: Exact for selected adult dyspepsia symptoms, H. pylori or endoscopy review, clinician-discussed management, alarm context, and safety-net documentation; examination and many retained symptoms are unsupported.
UAE_applicability: No exact UAE dyspepsia guideline was identified. NICE investigation, referral, and management pathways require UAE clinician review.
recency_verification: Current NICE CG184, last updated 2019-10-18, and current NICE NG12, last updated 2026-04-15, were reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: CG184 remains current and replaced CG17; NG12 remains current. No newer superseding NICE dyspepsia guideline was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports adult dyspepsia identity, epigastric pain or burning, reflux, bloating, nausea, precipitants, and clinician-discussed lifestyle or diet documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports adult recurrent epigastric pain, heartburn, acid regurgitation, bloating, nausea or vomiting, associated precipitants, meal timing, and clinician-discussed lifestyle documentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports significant acute GI-bleeding and reviewed prior-endoscopy fields without generating referral.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports immediate specialist referral for significant acute gastrointestinal bleeding, medicine-cause review, prior endoscopy review, and further assessment when new alarm signs are present.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports clinician-reviewed H. pylori testing context and follow-up review without ordering a test.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-reviewed H. pylori testing context and annual review for people needing long-term dyspepsia management; medicine names and doses are not mapped.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports clinician-decided medication and follow-up documentation without medicine names or doses.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented reflux management, medication-plan discussion, review, and specialist referral where symptoms do not respond or remain unexplained; no dose or autonomous treatment text is mapped.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports dysphagia and weight-loss warning fields only with recommendation-specific age and symptom qualifiers.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician recognition of dysphagia, weight-loss combinations, and jaundice in recommendation-specific age and symptom contexts; it does not assert cancer or an automatic referral decision.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports clinician-discussed return and agreed review if symptoms persist, recur, worsen, or cause concern.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting warning symptoms, when to return for re-evaluation, agreed review, and patient-initiated review if symptoms recur, persist, worsen, or cause concern.

unresolved_source_gaps: Functional dyspepsia, acid-stomach wording, post-prandial discomfort, early satiety, burping, reduced appetite, persistent vomiting, anaemia symptoms, severe pain, abdominal or hydration examination, blood tests, imaging, fixed one-to-two-week review, and the combined history draft remain unsupported.
The workflow must not generate a PPI, H. pylori test, endoscopy, antibiotic, medicine, investigation, referral, or alarm-feature conclusion.
Qualified UAE clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
